description_long: This object is a longsword with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a point, typical of European medieval swords. The blade is metallic and appears to be forged from steel, with a subtle fuller running down its length for added strength and reduced weight. The crossguard is straight and simple, made of polished metal, providing hand protection and balance. The handle is cylindrical and dark, likely wrapped in leather or a similar material for grip, ending in a rounded pommel that may be used for counterbalance or striking. The overall design is functional and unadorned, with no visible engravings or decorative elements. The sword is well-proportioned and appears suitable for both display and practical use in historical reenactments or as a prop.
description: A straight, double-edged longsword with a metallic blade, simple crossguard, and a dark cylindrical handle ending in a rounded pommel. The design is functional and unadorned.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with minimal width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and pommel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, dark, possibly leather-wrapped, rounded pommel
